functions.name: memory_store
functions.description: Store information in long-term memory for later recall. Use this when the user shares facts, preferences, decisions, or any information worth remembering.

key.description: Unique identifier for this memory. Use snake_case format (e.g., 'user_preference_theme', 'project_alpha_deadline')

description.description: Human-readable description of what this memory contains

data.description: Optional structured data to store alongside the description

functions.name: memory_recall
functions.description: Search memory for relevant information. Use this to find context from previous conversations or stored facts.

query.description: What to search for in memory. Can be keywords, phrases, or questions.

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return

max_depth.description: How many relationship links to follow when finding related information

functions.name: memory_update
functions.description: Update existing information in memory. Use when information has changed or needs correction.

key.description: The key of the memory to update

data.description: New structured data (merged with existing data)

functions.name: memory_forget
functions.description: Remove information from memory. Use when the user asks to delete something or information is no longer relevant.

key.description: The key of the memory to delete

cascade.description: If true, also remove relationships connected to this memory

functions.name: memory_get_overview
functions.description: Get a summary of what's currently stored in memory. Use at the start of conversations to understand available context.

functions.name: memory_find_related
functions.description: Find information related to a specific memory. Use to explore connections between stored information.

key.description: The key of the memory to find relationships for

max_depth.description: How many relationship links to follow

functions.name: memory_add_relationships
functions.description: Create connections between memories. Use to link related information together.

nodes.description: The two memory keys to connect
